questions.question: Explain the concept of 8-way symmetry in circle drawing algorithms. Why is it important?
keyPoints: Circle has uniform curvature in all octants
8-way symmetry means computing 1/8 of points and mirroring
Reduces computational work by factor of 8
Reflect across x-axis, y-axis, and line y=x

questions.question: Derive the initial decision parameter P₀ for the Midpoint Circle Algorithm when starting from (0, r).
keyPoints: Use midpoint at (1, r-0.5)
Evaluate circle equation: f(x,y) = x² + y² - r²
Simplify to P₀ = 1 - r

questions.question: What is aliasing? How does it affect circle drawing on raster displays?
keyPoints: Aliasing is the stair-step effect or jaggies
Occurs because continuous shapes map to discrete pixels
Circle edges appear jagged rather than smooth
Can be reduced by anti-aliasing techniques

questions.question: State the difference between Region 1 and Region 2 in the Midpoint Ellipse Algorithm.
keyPoints: Region 1: slope magnitude > 1 (steeper than 45°)
Region 2: slope magnitude < 1 (shallower than 45°)
Different pixel selection rules in each region
Switch point where ellipse slope equals 1

questions.question: Compare the Polar Coordinate method with the Midpoint Circle Algorithm for drawing circles.
keyPoints: Polar: x=r×cos(θ), y=r×sin(θ), uses trigonometry
Midpoint: uses circle equation, integer arithmetic only
Polar produces non-uniform point spacing
Midpoint is faster and produces better results

questions.question: Why does an ellipse have only 4-way symmetry while a circle has 8-way symmetry?
keyPoints: Circle has uniform curvature everywhere
Ellipse has different curvatures along major and minor axes
Ellipse shape differs in different octants
Only 4 reflections preserve ellipse shape: (±x, ±y)

questions.question: Write the recurrence relation for updating the decision parameter P in the Midpoint Circle Algorithm for both cases.
keyPoints: Case 1 (Pk < 0): P(k+1) = Pk + 2x + 3, select (x+1, y)
Case 2 (Pk ≥ 0): P(k+1) = Pk + 2x - 2y + 5, select (x+1, y-1)
x is incremented at each step
y is decremented only in Case 2

questions.question: Explain the Midpoint Circle Algorithm in detail. Derive the initial decision parameter and write the algorithm steps.
keyPoints: Start at (0, r) position
Use circle equation f(x,y) = x² + y² - r²
Initial P₀ = 1 - r
For each point: if Pk < 0, choose (x+1, y); else choose (x+1, y-1)
Update P accordingly using 2x+3 or 2x-2y+5
Use 8-way symmetry to plot all points
Continue until x ≥ y

questions.question: Explain the Midpoint Ellipse Algorithm with Region 1 and Region 2 operations. How does it handle the 4-way symmetry of an ellipse?
keyPoints: Uses ellipse equation b²x² + a²y² - a²b² = 0
Initial point (0, b), initial parameter
Region 1: x increments, choose based on P1k
Boundary condition: 2b²(x+1) > 2a²(y-0.5) - a²
Region 2: y increments, choose based on P2k
4-way symmetry: plot (±x, ±y) points only
Continue until y reaches 0

questions.question: Compare Bresenham's Circle Algorithm with the Midpoint Circle Algorithm. Are they equivalent? Justify your answer.
keyPoints: Both use integer arithmetic only
Both produce identical results
Midpoint uses circle equation at midpoint
Bresenham compares squared distances directly
Initial parameters differ: Midpoint P₀ = 1-r, Bresenham P₀ = 3-2r
Both achieve optimal pixel placement
Essentially equivalent in results and efficiency

questions.question: Draw a flowchart/algorithm to generate points for a circle using the Midpoint Circle Algorithm for radius r = 8. Show all intermediate decision parameter values.
keyPoints: Initialize x=0, y=r=8
Calculate P₀ = 1-r = 1-8 = -7
Plot initial point (0,8) and symmetry points
Iterate while x < y
Update P and (x,y) according to rules
Show at least 5-6 iterations with P values
Stop when x ≥ y
